headline: China’s Victory Giant Seeks Up to $2.2 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. began taking investor orders for a listing in Hong Kong that may raise as much as HK$17.5 billion ($2.2 billion) in what is set to be among the city’s largest first-time share sales this year. The maker of printed circuit board products — the intricate electronic backbone of artificial-intelligence servers — is offering 83.3 million shares at as much as HK$209.88 each, according to a listing document released on Monday. headline: How The Liberty Global (LBTYA) Investment Story Is Shifting With Fresh Analyst Targets
summary: The latest research refresh on Liberty Global nudges fair value estimates slightly higher, with one model moving from US$15.84 to US$15.89 per share and other commentary pointing to targets around US$14. These fine tuning moves are being framed by analysts as adjustments to existing views rather than a reset of the story, with modest changes in targets tied to refreshed assumptions after recent results. As you read on, you will see how these numbers feed into the evolving analyst narrative...
